com.newland.nsdk.core.api.internal.emvl2.service

Interface EMVL2Service

  • All Superinterfaces:
    Module

    public interface EMVL2Service
extends Module
    Provides EMV L2 related functions.

    • Method Detail

      • NAPIEMVL2SetCallbackFunction

        int NAPIEMVL2SetCallbackFunction(EmvJNIListener callbackFunction)
        Sets EMV event listener.
        Parameters:
        callbackFunction - [IN] EMV event listener. See EmvJNIListener.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

        int NAPIEMVL2FreeGlobalVariable()

      • NAPIEMVL2BuildCandidateList

        int NAPIEMVL2BuildCandidateList(java.util.ArrayList<candidate_emv> candidateList,
                                java.util.ArrayList<aidlist_emv> aidList)
        Builds candidate list by PSE or AID selection method.
        Parameters:
        candidateList - [OUT] Candidate list after building.
        aidList - [IN] Terminal AID list.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NEED_CONFIRM: Success, candidate list needs to be confirmed by cardholder.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_AUTO_SELECT: Success, no need to confirm candidate list by cardholder.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Any params errors.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_CARD_BLOCK: ICC response 6A81 which means the card is blocked when PSE selection.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_APP_BLOCK: ICC response 6283 which means the application is blocked when AID selection.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: There are no mutually supported applications or ICC error, the application should terminate the transaction.

      • NAPIEMVL2SelectApplication

        int NAPIEMVL2SelectApplication(int selectionID,
                               java.util.ArrayList<candidate_emv> candidateList)
        Selects the application from the candidate list by auto selection or cardholder confirmation.
        Parameters:
        selectionID - [IN] The index of candidate list to be selected. 0: auto selection.
        candidateList - [IN/OUT] The candidate list after final selection. If application selection failed, related candidate list will be removed, candidate list will be updated.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Application selection successful.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Any params errors.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_APP_BLOCK: ICC response 6283 which means the application is blocked when AID selection.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NO_MORE_APPS: Before final selection or after selection failed, there is no more application in candidate list. The terminal application should terminate the transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_SELECT_NEXT: After selection failed, candidate list still has applications. The terminal application can continue perform auto selection or cardholder confirmation.

      • NAPIEMVL2InitiateApplication

        int NAPIEMVL2InitiateApplication(java.util.ArrayList<candidate_emv> candidateList)
        Sends "Get Process Option (GPO)" command to IC card to initiate application processing.
        Parameters:
        candidateList - Candidate list.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM:Any one of mandatory terminal TAGs about 9F33, 9F35 or 9F40 is missing.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_SELECT_NEXT: The card returns SW1 SW2 = '6985' in response to GET PROCESSING OPTIONS command, the terminal should display "NOT ACCEPTED" message and shall return to application selection.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NO_MORE_APPS: The card returns SW1 SW2 = '6985' in response to GET PROCESSING OPTIONS command, and there is no more application in the candidate list, the terminal application should terminate the session.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: The card returns SW1 SW2 not '6985' or '9000', 77 or 80 template parse error, AIP or AFL missing or ICC error, the application should terminate the session.

      • NAPIEMVL2ReadApplicationData

        int NAPIEMVL2ReadApplicationData()
        Reads application data according to the AFL. This is performed after the Initiate Application Processing function.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: AFL format error, or SW1 SW2 not '9000' in response to the READ RECORD command, or 70 template parse error, or mandatory data missing, or any ICC error, the application should terminate the transaction.

      • NAPIEMVL2OfflineDataAuthentication

        int NAPIEMVL2OfflineDataAuthentication(byte[] ddol,
                                       int ddolLen)
        Performs offline data authentication.

        Terminal application shall perform offline data authentication in any order after Read Application Data but before completion of terminal action analysis.

        Parameters:
        ddol - [IN] Default DDOL. If the termianl don't support DDA or default DDOL, passing NULL is OK.
        ddolLen - [IN] Default DDOL length.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Offline data authentication has been performed successfully.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Offline data authentication failed or not performed.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_CAPK_CHECKSUM: CAPK check failed, if operator action is needed, the terminal application shall display an error message.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: SW1 SW2 not '9000' in response to the INTERNAL AUTHENTICATE command, or 77 or 80 template parse error, or icc error, the application should terminate the session.

      • NAPIEMVL2ProcessingRestrictions

        int NAPIEMVL2ProcessingRestrictions()
        Checks Application Version Number, Application Usage Control, Application Effective/Expiration  Dates.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Restriction processing has been performed.

      • NAPIEMVL2CardholderVerification

        int NAPIEMVL2CardholderVerification(byte[] cvmCode,
                                    byte[] cvStatus)
        Performs cardholder verification. Analysis CVM list and returns current supported CVM code to the terminal to perform. If the CVM code is about PIN verification, the terminal should pass the result status by calling this API again.
        Parameters:
        cvmCode - Return current CVM code to the terminal to perform CV method.
        • If CVM code is EmvConst.EMVL2_CVM_CUSTOM_CONDITION, means kernel does not recognize CV condition code or CV rule, and application needs to process custom CV method.
        • If EmvConst.EMVL2_CVM_DONE, means cardholder verification completed and not need to call this API again.
        cvStatus -
        • [IN] Result of current CV method.
          • If current CV code is EmvConst.EMVL2_CVM_CUSTOM_CONDITION, terminal should return check result in byte 1 about EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_UNRECOGNIZE, EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_UNSUPPORT, EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_UNSATISFY, or EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_OK.
          • If current CV code is about offline PIN verify, terminal should return offline PIN verification result EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_OFFLINEPIN_VERIFICATION_OK, or EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_OFFLINEPIN_VERIFICATION_FAIL following two bytes status word that return in VERIFY command from IC card.
          • If previous CV code is EmvConst.EMVL2_CVM_ONLINE_PIN, terminal should return online PIN process result about EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_ONLINEPIN_OK.
        • [OUT] See below:
          • If CVM code is EmvConst.EMVL2_CVM_CUSTOM_CONDITION, it returns 10 bytes data. byte 1: CV rule. byte 2: CV condition. byte 3~6: Amount X. byte 7~10: Amount Y.
          • If CVM code is about PIN verify, first byte of cvStatus means PIN try counter.
          • If first byte of cvStatus is EmvConst.EMVL2_CVS_FAIL, means there is no more CV method can do and cardholder verification is failed, not need to call this API again.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Cardholder verification is performed.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: cvmCode or cvStatus is NULL pointer.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: Encounters formatting errors in CVM List such as a list with an odd number of bytes, Status word is not 9000, 63C0, 6983, 6984 or any error undefined in response to the VERIFY command, or any ICC error, the application should terminate the session.

      • NAPIEMVL2RiskManagement

        int NAPIEMVL2RiskManagement(int blackCard,
                            int forceOnline,
                            byte[] logAmount,
                            int targetPercentage,
                            int maxTargetPercentage,
                            byte[] thresholdValue)
        Performs terminal risk management.
        Parameters:
        blackCard - [IN] 0: the card absent on the blacklist. 1: the card presented on the blacklist.
        forceOnline - [IN] If the merchant forces the transaction online. 0: No. 1: Yes.
        logAmount - [IN] The transaction amount of the card has completed before, format n6.
        targetPercentage - [IN] Target Percentage to be used for Random Selection. (in the range of 0 to 99)
        maxTargetPercentage - [IN] Maximum Target Percentage to be used for Biased Random Selection. (also in the range of 0 to 99)
        thresholdValue - [IN] Threshold Value for Biased Random Selection (which must be zero or a positive number less than the floor limit), format n4.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Risk management has been performed.

      • NAPIEMVL2TerminalActionAnalysis

        int NAPIEMVL2TerminalActionAnalysis(int[] acType,
                                    byte[] tacDenial,
                                    byte[] tacOnline,
                                    byte[] tacDefault,
                                    int disableOnline,
                                    byte[] tdol,
                                    int tdolLen)
        Performs terminal action analysis. The terminal application should require AC according to the return value of acType by performing card action analysis.
        Parameters:
        acType - [OUT] The request AC type by the result of terminal action analysis.
        tacDenial - [IN] Terminal Action Code - Denial, 5 bytes.
        tacOnline - [IN] Terminal Action Code - Online, 5 bytes.
        tacDefault - [IN] Terminal Action Code - Default, 5 bytes.
        disableOnline - [IN] 0: ignore. 1: the terminal is for any reason disable to process the transaction online.
        tdol - [IN] Default TDOL.
        tdolLen - [IN] Default TDOL length.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success, the terminal should check or change acType before GAC.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Any parameter errors.

      • NAPIEMVL2GenerateAC1st

        int NAPIEMVL2GenerateAC1st(int[] advice,
                           int reqACType)
        Sends first Generate AC command to ICC to perform card action analysis.

        CDA signature should be required if terminal and ICC supported both. The terminal application checks return value to obtain the card's decision of offline approval, offline declined or online request. If ICC requested advice, the terminal application should send an advice message to the issuer.

        Parameters:
        reqACType - [IN] Terminal final decision of AC type.
        advice - [OUT] 0: No 1: Yes.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: 77 or 80 template parse error, or CID format error, or icc error, the application should terminate the session.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_GO_ONLINE: The terminal should process transaction online.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_DECLINE: The terminal should decline the transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_ACCEPT: The terminal should accept the transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_GAC2_AAC: The terminal should request AAC by calling NAPIEMVL2GenerateAC2nd(int[], int, byte[], int, byte[], int, byte[], int[]).

      • NAPIEMVL2GenerateAC2nd

        int NAPIEMVL2GenerateAC2nd(int[] advice,
                           int reqACType,
                           byte[] script71,
                           int script71Len,
                           byte[] script72,
                           int script72Len,
                           byte[] scriptResult,
                           int[] scriptResultLen)
        Sends second Generate AC command to ICC to perform card action analysis.

        If online processing performed after NAPIEMVL2GenerateAC1st(int[], int), terminal application analyzes the issuer authentication response code and make the final decision of reqACType. Authentication Response Code (TAG 8A) should be updated by application. If issuer returns Issuer Authentication Data (TAG 91), the application should also store first for issuer authentication. If online is disabled, NAPIEMVL2TerminalActionAnalysis(int[], byte[], byte[], byte[], int, byte[], int) should be called first to make the final decision before calling this function. After this function is called, terminal application checks the return value to obtain the card decision of TC approval or AAC declined. If ICC requests advice, terminal application should send a advice message to the issuer.

        Parameters:
        advice - [OUT] 0: No 1: Yes.
        reqACType - [IN] Online result or issuer voice result of the issuer. If online is disabled, assign second TAA result or direct request decision of AC type. CDA signature always be required if reqACType is TC.
        script71 - [IN] Type 71 issuer script.
        script71Len - [IN] The length of script71.
        script72 - [IN] Type 72 issuer script.
        script72Len - [IN] The length of script72.
        scriptResult - [OUT] Return the script result.
        scriptResultLen - [IN/OUT] The size of buffer scriptResult/The actual length of script result.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Any parameter errors.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FORMAT: There is no Issuer Script Command(tag 86) in script.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TERMINATE: 77 or 80 template parse error, or CID format error, or icc error, the application should terminate the session.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_DECLINE: The terminal should decline the transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_ACCEPT: The terminal should accept the transaction.

      • NAPIEMVL2InitTagData

        int NAPIEMVL2InitTagData()
        Clears all the tag data in kernel data store space.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

      • NAPIEMVL2ExistTag

        int NAPIEMVL2ExistTag(int tag)
        Checks if the tag exist in the kernel data space.
        Parameters:
        tag - [IN] The tag to check.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Present.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TAG_ABSENT: Absent.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetPinpk

        int NAPIEMVL2GetPinpk(publickey pinPK)
        Gets PIN encryption PK for offline encryption PIN verification.
        Parameters:
        pinPK - [OUT] PIN encryption  PK structure.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

      • NAPIEMVL2Initialize

        int NAPIEMVL2Initialize()
        Clears all tag data, initializes kernel status and clears kernel configuration.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

      • NAPIEMVL2SetConfig

        int NAPIEMVL2SetConfig(int opt,
                       int val)
        Sets kernel configuration.
        Parameters:
        opt - [IN] Configuration bitmap as macro defined: EMVL2_SUPPORT_XXX
        val - [IN] 0: Unset, 1: Set.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetConfig

        int NAPIEMVL2GetConfig(int opt)
        Gets kernel current configuration.
        Parameters:
        opt - [IN] Configuration bitmap as macro defined: EMVL2_SUPPORT_XXX
        Returns:
        • 0: unset.
        • 1: set.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetVersion

        java.lang.String NAPIEMVL2GetVersion()
        Gets EMV level 2 version information.
        Returns:
        Version.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetConfigChecksum

        int NAPIEMVL2GetConfigChecksum(byte[] checksum,
                               int size)
        Gets kernel configuration checksum.
        Parameters:
        checksum - [OUT] Kernel configuration checksum.
        size - [IN] The buffer size of checksum. Minimum value is 4.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: checksum is NULL, or size is less than 4 bytes.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL

      • NAPIEMVL2GetKernelChecksum

        int NAPIEMVL2GetKernelChecksum(byte[] checksum,
                               int size)
        Gets kernel checksum.
        Parameters:
        checksum - [OUT] Kernel checksum.
        size - [IN] The buffer size of checksum. Minimum value is 4.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: checksum is NULL, or size is less than 4 bytes.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL

      • NAPICLL2PerformTransaction

        int NAPICLL2PerformTransaction(ep_opt obj_epopt,
                               rf_transdata obj_rfdata,
                               byte[] ctrl,
                               byte[] processData,
                               int processDataLen)
        Contactless transaction processing.
        Parameters:
        obj_epopt - [IN/OUT] Entry_Point Trading Options.
        obj_rfdata - [IN/OUT] Trans Data Options.
        ctrl - [IN] Some control data.
        • byte 1: Card seeking flag
          • NO SEEK CARD =0
          • SEEK CARD IN APP = 1
          • SEEK CARD IN SERVER = 2
          • ACTIVE CARD IN SERVER = 3
        • byte 2: qPBOC getting data flag
          • NO NEED TO GET DATA = 0
          • NEED TO GET DATA = 1
        • byte 3: Process light flag(see the below, it needs to set all four lights status)
        • byte 4: Card reading OK light flag(see the below, it needs to set all four lights status)
          • bit 8-7 first light:
            • LED_RFID_BLUE_ON = 0x40
            • LED_RFID_BLUE_OFF = 0x80
            • LED_RFID_BLUE_FLICK = 0xc0
          • bit 6-5 third light:
            • LED_RFID_GREEN_ON = 0x10
            • LED_RFID_GREEN_OFF = 0x20
            • LED_RFID_GREEN_FLICK = 0x30
          • bit 4-3 second light:
            • LED_RFID_YELLOW_ON = 0x04
            • LED_RFID_YELLOW_OFF = 0x08
            • LED_RFID_YELLOW_FLICK = 0x0c
          • bit 2-1 four light:
            • LED_RFID_RED_ON = 0x01
            • LED_RFID_RED_OFF = 0x02
            • LED_RFID_RED_FLICK = 0x03
        • byte 5: After Final Selection callback flag
          • NO NEED CALLBACK = 0
          • NEED CALLBACK = 1
          • NAPI_EMV_BEFKRNEEDCALLBACK = 2
        processData - [IN] TLV transaction data.
        processDataLen - [IN] TLV Date length.
        Returns:
        Transaction result

      • NAPICLL2EntryPointProcess

        int NAPICLL2EntryPointProcess(ep_opt obj_epopt,
                              rf_transdata obj_rfdata)
        Contactless transaction pre-processing.
        Parameters:
        obj_epopt - [IN] Entry_Point Transaction Options
        obj_rfdata - [IN] Contactless transaction mandatory data.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TERMINATE: Terminate a transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TRYOTHERINT: Try another interface.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_ACTIVE_KERNEL: According to the response of the corresponding kernel ID activation related transactions, continue to complete the transaction.

      • NAPICLL2PayPassProcess

        int NAPICLL2PayPassProcess(ep_opt obj_epopt,
                           rf_transdata obj_rfdata)
        Processes a Paypass transaction.
        Parameters:
        obj_epopt - [IN] Entry_Point Transaction Options.
        obj_rfdata - [IN] Contactless transaction mandatory data.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TERMINATE: Terminate a transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_ACCEPT: M/CHIP Offline Approve.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_DENIAL: CLSS M/CHIP decline.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_GOONLINE: CLSS M/CHIP Online Request.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_GOONLINE: CLSS MAG Online Request.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_ACCEPT: CLSS MAG Approve.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TRYOTHERINT: Try another interface.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_SELECT_NEXT_AID: Select next aid.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_DENIAL: CLSS MAG Decline.

      • NAPICLL2PayWaveProcess

        int NAPICLL2PayWaveProcess(ep_opt obj_epopt,
                           rf_transdata obj_rfdata)
        Processes a Paywave transaction.   
        Parameters:
        obj_epopt - [IN] Entry_Point Transaction Options.
        obj_rfdata - [IN] Contactless transaction mandatory data.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TERMINATE: Terminate a transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_ACCEPT: M/CHIP Offline Approve.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_DENIAL: CLSS M/CHIP decline.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_GOONLINE: CLSS M/CHIP Online Request.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_GOONLINE: CLSS MAG Online Request.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_ACCEPT: CLSS MAG Approve.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TRYOTHERINT: Try another interface.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_DOWNCARD: See phone.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_SELECT_NEXT_AID: Select next aid.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_DENIAL: CLSS MAG Decline.

        int NAPICLL2EntryPointSuspend(int nTransFinal)
        Finishes a contactless transaction.
        Parameters:
        nTransFinal - [IN] Final transaction result.
        Returns:
        • 0: Success.
        • -1: Failed.

      • NAPICLL2PayPassSuspend

        Parameters:

        Returns:
        • 0: Success.
        • -1: Failed.

        • 0: Success.
        • -1: Failed.

      • NAPICLL2ExpressPaySuspend

        int NAPICLL2ExpressPaySuspend(int nTransFinal)
        Finishes an ExpressPay transaction.
        Parameters:
        nTransFinal - [IN] Final result of a transaction (e.g., EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_ACCEPT, EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_DENIAL).
        Returns:
        • 0: Success.
        • -1: Failed.

      • NAPIEntryPointGetKernelChecksum

        int NAPIEntryPointGetKernelChecksum(byte[] checksum,
                                    int size)
        Gets EntryPoint Kernel Checksum.
        Parameters:
        checksum - [OUT] Kernel checksum.
        size - [IN] The buffer size of checksum. It shall be >=4.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Parameter error.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Parameter error.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

        byte[] NAPIEMVL2GetStatusData()
        Gets status data.

        int NAPIEMVL2GetErrorCode()
        Gets detailed transaction error code.

        Error code.

      • NAPIEMVL2PackTLV

        int NAPIEMVL2PackTLV(byte[] outBuf,
                     int[] bufLen,
                     int tag,
                     int tagLen,
                     byte[] tagValue,
                     int type)
        Packs data by EMV TLV struct conventions.
        Parameters:
        outBuf - [OUT] The buffer pointer of TLV packing data to store.
        bufLen - [IN/OUT] The size of TLV buffer./The actual size of the TLV packing data.
        tag - [IN] Tag definition.
        tagLen - [IN] The length of tag value.
        tagValue - [IN] Tag value.
        type - [IN] Reserved.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Any parameter error.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_OVERFLOW: The size of buffer is too small.

      • NAPIEMVL2PackTagDataToTLV

        int NAPIEMVL2PackTagDataToTLV(byte[] outBuf,
                              int[] bufLen,
                              int[] tagList,
                              int tagNum,
                              int control)
        Gets the TLV data of specified tags.
        Parameters:
        outBuf - [OUT] The output TLV data.
        bufLen - [IN/OUT] The max length of TLV buffer/The actual length of TLV buffer.
        tagList - [IN] The tag list which will be packed to TLV buffer.
        tagNum - [IN] The number of tags.
        control - [IN] 0: Not pack empty tag to TLV buffer. 1: Pack empty tag with format "TL" to TLV buffer.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Any parameter error.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_OVERFLOW: The size of TLV buffer is too small.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetData

        int NAPIEMVL2GetData(int tagName,
                     byte[] outData,
                     int maxOutLen)
        Finds the tag and gets its value.
        Parameters:
        tagName - [IN] The tag to get value.
        outData - [OUT] The buffer used to store the tag's value.
        maxOutLen - [IN] The max size of the buffer.
        Returns:
        • >= 0: The actual length of the tag value.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Buffer is NULL or max length is 0.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TAG_ABSENT: Tag is absent.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_OVERFLOW: The length of the tag value is greater than max length.

      • NAPIEMVL2SetData

        int NAPIEMVL2SetData(int tagName,
                     byte[] data,
                     int maxLen)
        Sets value to the specified tag.
        Parameters:
        tagName - [IN] The tag to set.
        data - [IN] The value set to the tag.
        maxLen - [IN] The size of the buffer.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Buffer is NULL.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TAG_UNKNOWN: Unknown tag.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_TAG_REPEAT: The tag is already exist.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_OVERFLOW: Kernel data space is overflow.

      • NAPIEMVL2ICCGetData

        int NAPIEMVL2ICCGetData(int emgetdata,
                        byte[] dataout,
                        int[] valuelen)
        Gets Data.
        Parameters:
        emgetdata -
        dataout -
        valuelen -
        Returns:
        Result code.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetPBOCLog

        int NAPIEMVL2GetPBOCLog(int nRec,
                        byte[] pusOut,
                        int nOutMaxLen)
        Gets EMV Transaction Detail.
        Parameters:
        nRec - [IN] See below:
        • >0: Number of records to read.
        • =PBOCLOG_SFI: transaction detail SFI.
        • =PBOCLOG_RECNUM: Number of records.
        • =PBOCLOG_FMT: Transaction detail format.
        pusOut - [OUT] Transaction detail data.
        nOutMaxLen - [IN] Max len of transaction detail data.
        Returns:
        • <0: Failed.
        • >0: Transaction detail data length.
        • =0: No detail data.

      • NAPIEMVL2GetecloadLog

        int NAPIEMVL2GetecloadLog(int nRec,
                          byte[] pusOut,
                          int nOutMaxLen)
        Gets EMV ECLoad Log.
        Parameters:
        nRec - [IN] See below:
        • >0: Number of records to read.
        • =PBOCLOG_SFI: ECLoad log SFI.
        • =PBOCLOG_RECNUM: Number of records.
        • =PBOCLOG_FMT: ECLoad log format.
        pusOut - [OUT] ECLoad log data.
        nOutMaxLen - [IN] Max length of ECLoad log data.
        Returns:
        • <0: Failed.
        • >0: CLoad log data len.
        • =0: No log.

        byte[] NAPICLL2EntryPointGetCLL2Status()
        Gets contactless level 2 status.
        Returns:
        Contactless level2 status.

      • NAPICLL2EntryPointGetDataByTagName

        int NAPICLL2EntryPointGetDataByTagName(int tagName,
                                       byte[] dataOut,
                                       int[] dataOutLen)
        Uses GET DATA Command to get value of the Tag.
        Parameters:
        tagName - [IN] TAG name, i.e: 0x9F36
        dataOut - [OUT] Out Value.
        dataOutLen - [OUT] Out length.
        Returns:
        • 0: Success.
        • -1: Failed.

      • NAPIEMVL2SetDebugMode

        void NAPIEMVL2SetDebugMode(int debugLv)
        Sets Kernel debug mode.
        Parameters:
        debugLv - Debug level.
        • LV_CLOSE(0): Close debug.
        • LV_DEBUG(1): Log with normal debug information. This is recommended.
        • LV_ALL(3): Log with all of the debug information.

        void NAPICLL2SetIsNDKEMV(int isNDKEMV)

        int NAPIEMVSetKeytoPIN(int KeyValue)
        Sets key to PIN.
        Parameters:
        KeyValue -
        Returns:
        Result code.

      • NAPICLL2EntryPointGetData

        int NAPICLL2EntryPointGetData(int tagName,
                              byte[] outData,
                              int maxOutLen)
        Gets TLV value of the tag.
        Parameters:
        tagName - [IN] The tag to get.
        outData - [OUT] The value the tag.
        maxOutLen - [IN] Max length of the data.
        Returns:
        • 0: Tag is not exist.
        • -1: Data length exceeds length limit.
        • >0: the length of the value.

      • NAPICLL2EntryPointSetData

        int NAPICLL2EntryPointSetData(int tagName,
                              byte[] dataIn,
                              int maxLen)
        Sets the value to the tag.
        Parameters:
        tagName - [IN] The tag to set.
        dataIn - [IN] Value
        maxLen - [IN] Value Length
        Returns:
        • 0: Success.
        • <0: Failed.
        • -2: No permission for setting this tag.

      • NAPICLL2EntryPointFetchData

        int NAPICLL2EntryPointFetchData(byte[] outBuf,
                                int[] bufLen,
                                int[] tagList,
                                int tagNum,
                                int control)
        Packs tag data with tlv format.
        Parameters:
        outBuf - [OUT] The output packed data.
        bufLen - [IN] Maximum length of the TLV buffer.
        tagList - [IN] Tags to get value and packed to the TLV buffer.
        tagNum - [IN] The number of tags.
        control - [IN] 0: Not pack empty tag to the TLV buffer. 1: Pack empty tag with format "TL" to the TLV buffer.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: TLV buffer is null or max buffer length is 0.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_OVERFLOW: Buffer length is too small to store the packed TLV data.

      • NAPICLL2GetErrorCode

        int NAPICLL2GetErrorCode()
        Gets detailed error code.
        Returns:
        Error code.

      • GetExceptionRemove

        int GetExceptionRemove()
        Gets IC Card Exception status
        Returns:
        • 0: Good status
        • 1: IC Card is removed/loose while transaction is performing

      • ResetExceptionRemove

        void ResetExceptionRemove()
        Resets IC Card Exception status.

        int NAPICLL2SetCustomerTagList(int[] tagList,
                               int tagNum)
        Sets the custom tag list
        Parameters:
        tagList -
        tagNum -
        Returns:
        0-SUCC, <0-FAIL

        int NAPICLL2GetCandidateCustomData(byte[] aid,
                                   int aidlen,
                                   byte[] customdata)
        Get the Candidate list custom tag value
        Parameters:
        aid -
        aidlen -
        customdata -
        Returns:
        0-SUCC, <0-FAIL

      • NAPICLL2GirocardProcess

        int NAPICLL2GirocardProcess(ep_opt obj_epopt,
                            rf_transdata obj_rfdata)
        Processes a Girocard transaction.
        Parameters:
        obj_epopt - [IN] Entry_Point Transaction Options.
        obj_rfdata - [IN] Contactless transaction mandatory data.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TERMINATE: Terminate a transaction.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_ACCEPT: M/CHIP Offline Approve.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_DENIAL: CLSS M/CHIP decline.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_GOONLINE: CLSS M/CHIP Online Request.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_GOONLINE: CLSS MAG Online Request.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_GOONLINE_LONGTAP: CLSS M.CHIP Online Request legacy card.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_GOONLINE_ONLINETAP: CLSS M.CHIP Online Request no legacy card.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_ACCEPT: CLSS MAG Approve.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_TRYOTHERINT: Try another interface.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_SELECT_NEXT_AID: Select next aid.
        • EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MAG_DENIAL: CLSS MAG Decline.

      • NAPICLL2GirocardSuspend

        int NAPICLL2GirocardSuspend(int nTransFinal)
        Finishes a Girocard transaction.
        Parameters:
        nTransFinal - [IN] Final result of a transaction (e.g., EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_ACCEPT, EmvConst.EMV_TRANS_RF_MCHIP_DENIAL).
        Returns:
        • 0: Success.
        • -1: Failed.

      • NAPICLL2GirocardGetKernelChecksum

        int NAPICLL2GirocardGetKernelChecksum(byte[] checksum,
                                      int size)
        Gets Girocard Kernel Checksum.
        Parameters:
        checksum - [OUT] Kernel checksum.
        size - [IN] The buffer size of checksum. It must be not less than 4.
        Returns:
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_NONE: Success.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_PARAM: Parameter error.
        • EmvConst.EMVL2_ERR_FAIL: Failed.

      • NAPICLL2GirocardGetVersion

        java.lang.String NAPICLL2GirocardGetVersion()
        Gets Girocard Kernel version.
        Returns:
        Version Girocard version.

      • getErrorMessage

        java.lang.String getErrorMessage(int emvErrorCode)
        Gets error message according to the error code.
        Parameters:
        emvErrorCode - EMV error code.
        Returns:
        Error message
